response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is an accomplished full-stack and systems engineer with a broad portfolio, but he falls critically short of the role's core requirements. The position demands 8 years of C++ experience, yet the resume shows only a brief ~6-month stint writing C/C++ components at National Instruments and a single academic project. His primary languages are TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang. While he has touched distributed systems and cloud tooling (microservices handling millions of requests/sec at Dunnhumby, AWS CDK at Optilyz, Kubernetes for GISCollective), this does not constitute the 5 years of large-scale infrastructure or cloud compute platform leadership the role requires. His background skews toward web/product engineering, not systems-level cloud infrastructure.
strengths: Genuine distributed-systems exposure: microservices at Dunnhumby handling millions of HTTP requests/sec demonstrates understanding of high-throughput, low-latency design.
Strong culture of engineering craft: consistent use of TDD, trunk-based development, CI/CD, and community knowledge-sharing (workshops, dojos) signals mentoring potential.
Broad polyglot and platform experience across embedded, mobile, web, and infrastructure tooling shows adaptability and systems thinking beyond a single stack.
concerns: C++ depth is negligible: 8 years proficiency is the hard minimum requirement; the resume shows roughly 6 months of C/C++ usage over a 16-year career.
No cloud compute platform or large-scale infrastructure ownership: AWS CDK for a direct-mail pricing service and a Kubernetes-deployed OSS GIS tool do not satisfy the 5-year infrastructure bar.

No demonstrated technical leadership in a cloud or DevOps context: workshop facilitation and open-source maintainership do not substitute for leading engineering teams on production platform strategy.
